commands.review_summary:
Adds a chat.history projection branch that hides a subagent_announce inter-session user message together with its adjacent assistant reply, plus regression tests for provenance, inline-prefix, and trailing-announce cases.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +77. Total +89 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main sends chat.history rows through projectRecentChatDisplayMessages, and the existing filter hides the subagent_announce user row but not the adjacent assistant row. I did not run a live reproduction because this review is read-only and local tests would write artifacts.
commands.review_followup: This PR needs contributor runtime proof or maintainer proof override before merge, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(gateway): hide subagent_announce assistant reply alongside its inter-session user message
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: After `/new` on a feishu (or any) channel that previously received a `subagent_announce` delivery under the same `sessionKey`, the runtime's `sessions_history(sessionKey=…)` call hydrates the new conversation with the trailing assistant reply from the long-finished announce turn. The assistant model treats it as "the most recent thing the user just said" and resumes an unrelated topic. (#85648)
- **Root Cause**: `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` already hides the user-role inter-session announce message via `shouldHideProjectedHistoryMessage` → `isSubagentAnnounceInterSessionUserMessage` (matches `provenance.kind=inter_session` + `sourceTool=subagent_announce`, or the inline `[Inter-session message] sourceTool=subagent_announce` prefix). Its immediately-following assistant reply carries no announce-specific signal of its own — it is just the agent's text response to that announce turn — so it falls through every filter and lands in the chat-history response, exactly the "fanfic lore-bible summary" leak the reporter observed inside a fresh paper-defense conversation.
- **Fix**: In `filterVisibleProjectedHistoryMessages`, treat the (user inter-session announce, immediately-following assistant) pair as a hidden unit — same pattern as the existing heartbeat-ack pair-hide just above. The user-role match condition is the existing `isSubagentAnnounceInterSessionUserMessage(current)` so detection is consistent with the per-message hide path; the new branch only fires when the next message is role `assistant`. Trailing announces with no paired assistant still fall through to the existing user-only filter and are hidden there.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts`: one additional branch (12 lines) inside the existing `filterVisibleProjectedHistoryMessages` iteration, between the heartbeat pair-hide and the per-message `shouldHideProjectedHistoryMessage` check.
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`: three new cases under `describe("projectRecentChatDisplayMessages", …)` — provenance-tagged announce pair surrounded by real turns, text-prefix-tagged announce pair (no `provenance` field), trailing announce without an assistant reply.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Detection logic for the user-role announce (`isSubagentAnnounceInterSessionUserMessage`, `shouldHideProjectedHistoryMessage`) is unchanged; the new branch reuses it.
  - The transcript persistence path, the session-store entry resolution, the `chat.history` sessionKey→sessionId lookup, and the announce-delivery runner are unchanged.
  - No other projection branch (heartbeat, ACP duplicate, runtime-context, oversized) is touched.
  - No config keys, protocol, or message-delivery behavior changed.

### Reproduction

1. From a feishu (or any) direct chat, spawn a subagent that finishes a task and emits `subagent_announce`. The announce is persisted under the same `sessionKey` as the user's main session (an inter-session user message + the agent's assistant reply).
2. Days later, issue `/new` on that chat. The runtime auto-calls `sessions_history(sessionKey=…)` shortly after `session.started`.
- Before: the announce assistant reply leaks into the `chat.history` response (in the reporter's trace at `seq=2`, alongside the announce user message at `seq=1`); the model resumes the unrelated subagent topic.
- After: the announce user message AND its assistant reply are both stripped from the projected history; the new session is hydrated only with real prior turns (or empty if none).

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed (#85648)**: when chat.history reads a session transcript that contains an inter-session `subagent_announce` turn, both the announce user message AND its adjacent assistant reply must be hidden from default hydration; surrounding real user/assistant turns must still come through unaffected.
- **Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22)**: targeted Vitest run against the real `projectRecentChatDisplayMessages` (the same function `chat.history` invokes), exercising the provenance-tagged, text-prefix-tagged, and trailing-announce shapes the announce-delivery path actually produces in the JSONL transcript.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts -t "projectRecentChatDisplayMessages"`.
- **Evidence after fix (verbatim test output)**:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  13 passed | 93 skipped (106)
  Start at  20:25:40
  Duration  18.54s
```

- **Observed result after fix**: the three new announce-pair cases pass — the provenance-tagged case asserts a 5-turn transcript (real user → real assistant → announce user → announce assistant → next user) projects to exactly 3 turns (real user, real assistant, next user) with both announce messages stripped and the surrounding real turns intact; the text-prefix case asserts the same pair-hide when only the inline `[Inter-session message] sourceTool=subagent_announce` text is present and `provenance` is missing; the trailing-announce case asserts the user-only fall-through still removes a final announce when no assistant follows. The ten pre-existing `projectRecentChatDisplayMessages` cases (heartbeat ack pair-hide, ACP duplicate suppression, runtime-context hidden, TTS supplement merge, media-only preservation, applies-history-limits-after-hide, etc.) still pass unchanged — no regression in any sibling projection branch.
- **Regression tests**: the three new cases cover both detection branches (`provenance.kind` and inline prefix text) and the trailing-no-assistant edge; the surrounding-real-turns assertion in the provenance case pins that the new branch cannot accidentally skip an unrelated user message adjacent to the announce.
- **What was not tested**: a live end-to-end gateway run with a real feishu announce-delivery turn was not executed. The projection function is pure (no I/O, no clock, no async), and the unit tests reproduce the exact `chat.history` input shape including both the `provenance` field and the inline `[Inter-session message] sourceTool=subagent_announce` prefix variant the announce-delivery path persists.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: the new branch could over-hide a regular user→assistant turn pair.
- **Mitigation**: the branch is gated on `isSubagentAnnounceInterSessionUserMessage(current)` AND `nextRoleContent.role === "assistant"`. A regular user message cannot satisfy `isSubagentAnnounceInterSessionUserMessage` — it requires `provenance.kind=inter_session && provenance.sourceTool=subagent_announce`, or both `[Inter-session message]` and `sourceTool=subagent_announce` to appear in the message text. The provenance-tagged test asserts the `next user turn` real message at `timestamp: 5` survives unchanged (so the new branch only consumes the (user-announce, assistant) pair, not the real user that follows); the existing "applies history limits after dropping display-hidden messages" case still passes, so the broader display-hide pipeline is unchanged.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #85648
